phenotypes.label: Type 2 diabetes with cardiometabolic comorbidities
phenotypes.description: Patients with type 2 diabetes clustered with cardiometabolic comorbidities: essential hypertension, hyperlipidemia, obesity, obstructive sleep apnea, and GERD. Musculoskeletal/metabolic issues (osteoporosis, vitamin D deficiency), chronic pain, anemia, chest pain or dyspnea, and mood/sleep disorders are also represented. Cognitive complaints including amnesia and dementia diagnoses occur within this cluster.

phenotypes.label: Type 2 diabetes mellitus
phenotypes.description: This cluster is dominated by type 2 diabetes mellitus. Associated conditions include hyperlipidemia and hypertension, with paresthesias suggestive of diabetic neuropathy, alongside nonspecific pain and gastrointestinal complaints.

phenotypes.label: Influenza/COVID with asthma exacerbation
phenotypes.description: Acute viral respiratory illnesses, notably influenza and COVID-19, cluster with asthma and lower-airway symptoms. Presentations include wheeze, dyspnea, chest tightness or pain, rales, and respiratory distress, often with systemic or gastrointestinal symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain. Syncope and anxiety appear as associated acute manifestations.

phenotypes.label: Osteoporosis
phenotypes.description: The pattern is anchored by osteoporosis, indicating a bone‑fragility phenotype in older adults. Co-occurring musculoskeletal pain, joint pain, injury, anemia, and vitamin D deficiency reinforce skeletal vulnerability. Gastroesophageal symptoms (heartburn/GERD, nausea, constipation/diarrhea) and cardiopulmonary complaints (orthopnea, cyanosis) reflect common comorbid symptoms in this population.

phenotypes.label: Multimorbidity with nonspecific symptoms
phenotypes.description: General multimorbidity pattern combining cardiometabolic conditions (type 2 diabetes, hypertension, hyperlipidemia, obesity) and GERD, with anemia and sleep apnea. Presentations feature nonspecific symptoms—pain, chest pain, dyspnea, anxiety, nausea/vomiting, and bowel changes—reflecting a broad comorbidity burden rather than a single disease process in patients with dementia.

phenotypes.label: Anxiety, insomnia, dyspnea, and pain
phenotypes.description: Nonspecific symptom-driven presentations dominated by anxiety and insomnia alongside dyspnea/wheezing or asthma, joint pain and generalized pain, and gastrointestinal upset (nausea, vomiting, constipation). This pattern reflects broad symptom burden rather than a single disease entity.

phenotypes.label: Chronic comorbidities with somatic symptoms
phenotypes.description: Common chronic conditions (hypertension, type 2 diabetes) co-occur with nonspecific symptoms such as chest and abdominal pain, dyspnea, nausea/vomiting, pruritus, and generalized pain. Depression and anxiety are prominent, alongside geriatric features like dysphagia, osteoporosis, vitamin D deficiency, and weakness—reflecting a general medical baseline rather than a single disease process.

phenotypes.label: Amnesia and postconcussive syndrome
phenotypes.description: This cluster reflects amnestic syndromes with co-documented postconcussion syndrome in the context of early dementia. Mood and sleep disorders, seizures, and chronic pain co-occur, with prominent cardiometabolic comorbidity (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, diabetes, obesity).

phenotypes.label: Joint pain (arthralgia)
phenotypes.description: This theme is dominated by arthralgia, indicating patients presenting with joint pain complaints. Co-occurring conditions such as asthma/wheezing, GERD and heartburn, chest or abdominal pain, and anxiety/depression reflect accompanying comorbidity and symptom burden rather than a single organ‑specific disease.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are generic cross-system symptoms and common chronic diagnoses with no unifying clinical story. This reads as background comorbidity rather than a distinct condition cluster.

phenotypes.label: Unused low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: This grouping mixes nonspecific symptoms (wheezing, chest pain, nausea, diarrhea) with baseline comorbid conditions, without a single shared clinical theme. It is not useful for identifying a specific clinical cohort.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine generic symptoms (chest pain, shortness of breath, nausea, vomiting) and common chronic comorbidities (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, GERD, anxiety) without a unifying clinical diagnosis. This heterogeneous mix does not define a specific clinical phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions blend common cardiometabolic comorbidities (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, type 2 diabetes) with nonspecific symptoms such as pain, gastrointestinal upset, and dyspnea, plus sleep apnea. There is no unifying clinical mechanism or organ system, indicating a generic baseline rather than a distinct condition cluster.

phenotypes.label: Chronic comorbidity catch-all
phenotypes.description: Broad internal-medicine cluster of common chronic conditions: hypertension, hyperlipidemia, type 2 diabetes, GERD, obesity, and obstructive sleep apnea. Co-occurring pain, anxiety and depressive disorders, insomnia, anemia, and vitamin D deficiency are frequent. Postconcussive symptoms and amnesia also appear within this general comorbidity mix.

phenotypes.label: Postconcussion syndrome with chronic pain
phenotypes.description: Patients show postconcussion syndrome with prominent chronic pain. Associated features include memory complaints, insomnia, depression/anxiety, and additional pain syndromes (e.g., joint and chest pain), consistent with persistent post‑traumatic symptoms.

phenotypes.label: Seizures
phenotypes.description: Seizure events dominate, reflecting patients with recurrent convulsions. Co-occurring features include postictal headache, amnesia, tingling, insomnia, anxiety, and injuries from convulsions or falls, with nausea/vomiting and chest pain also appearing.

phenotypes.label: Unused low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions mix generic symptoms (pain, chest pain, nausea/vomiting, shortness of breath) with common chronic comorbidities (hypertension, type 2 diabetes, GERD, asthma, hypothyroidism, depression) and neurologic complaints (syncope, dysphagia, tingling). There is no single clinical pathway tying these together, so this set is not clinically cohesive.

phenotypes.label: Unused low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are generic symptoms (pain, chest pain, nausea) mixed with common chronic comorbidities (hypertension, diabetes, hyperlipidemia) and mood symptoms, without a single clinical theme. This pattern is not specific enough to define a coherent phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Unused, low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are generic acute symptoms and complaints (pain, chest pain, dyspnea, nausea, vomiting) seen across many encounters. There is no unifying disease mechanism or organ-system focus, so this does not define a distinct clinical phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Unused low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are generic chronic comorbidities and nonspecific symptoms (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, GERD, anxiety, pain) seen broadly across patients. There is no unifying clinical mechanism, so this pattern does not define a usable phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are a generic blend of hypertension, diabetes, anxiety/depression, and nonspecific cardiopulmonary or gastrointestinal symptoms. There is no unifying clinical theme, indicating general baseline comorbidity rather than a focused condition cluster.

phenotypes.label: Unused low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions blend generic symptoms (chest pain, dyspnea, nausea, vomiting) with common chronic comorbidities (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, obesity) and mood disorders without a unifying clinical syndrome. This pattern does not define a specific phenotype and is not clinically actionable on its own.

phenotypes.label: Chronic pain with depressive disorders
phenotypes.description: Chronic pain syndromes with prominent abdominal and generalized pain co-occur with depressive and anxiety disorders. Obesity and anemia are frequent, reflecting high somatic symptom burden and multimorbidity; asthma and bleeding symptoms appear as additional comorbidities.

phenotypes.label: Muscle weakness
phenotypes.description: This cluster is dominated by muscle weakness, with frequent accompanying neurologic complaints (neurological deficit, syncope, seizure, amnesia, post‑concussion symptoms) and generalized pain or cardiopulmonary discomfort. It reflects encounters documenting generalized or focal weakness in the setting of broad systemic symptoms and common chronic comorbidities such as diabetes and hypertension.

phenotypes.label: Anxiety disorders
phenotypes.description: Anxiety is the defining condition, with frequent co-occurring chronic pain, insomnia, sleep apnea, GERD/heartburn, and depressive symptoms. Somatic complaints such as chest pain, nausea, dysphagia, and joint pain commonly accompany the anxiety picture in these patients.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are a mix of nonspecific symptoms (pain, chest pain, dyspnea, nausea/vomiting/diarrhea) and common chronic comorbidities (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, anxiety, OSA) without a unifying clinical theme. This blend does not define a specific condition pattern for selection or analysis.

phenotypes.label: Generic symptoms/comorbidity blend
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are a mix of ubiquitous symptoms (pain, chest pain, dyspnea, nausea) and common chronic comorbidities (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, obesity, GERD) without a unifying clinical theme. This reflects general presentation patterns rather than a distinct condition cluster.

phenotypes.label: Unused low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions blend common chronic comorbidities (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, obesity) with nonspecific symptoms (pain, nausea, diarrhea) and scattered issues such as postconcussion syndrome. There is no unifying clinical pattern, so this grouping is not clinically actionable.

phenotypes.label: Acute symptom and comorbidity baseline
phenotypes.description: Nonspecific acute complaints dominate—chest pain, nausea, dyspnea, abdominal or generalized pain, and bleeding—sometimes alongside influenza and wheezing. These occur with common chronic comorbidities such as hypertension, hyperlipidemia, type 2 diabetes, asthma, and dementia, reflecting a general medical baseline rather than a single disease process.

phenotypes.label: Common symptoms with mood disorders
phenotypes.description: Patients present with nonspecific somatic complaints—itching, abdominal pain, dyspnea, chest pain, constipation, and generalized weakness—alongside depressive and anxiety disorders. Cardiometabolic comorbidities (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, obesity) and reflux symptoms are common, indicating routine primary care presentations rather than a single disease process.

phenotypes.label: Chronic and acute symptom catch-all
phenotypes.description: Blend of common cardiometabolic comorbidities (hyperlipidemia, hypertension) with nonspecific acute presentations such as chest pain, dyspnea, nausea/vomiting, and influenza with wheeze. This reflects broad baseline morbidity in older adults with dementia rather than a specific disease phenotype; osteoporosis and bleeding likely reflect age-related fragility and intercurrent events.

phenotypes.label: Chronic multimorbidity baseline
phenotypes.description: This cluster reflects common chronic multimorbidity: hypertension, hyperlipidemia, type 2 diabetes, obesity, sleep apnea, GERD, osteoporosis, vitamin D deficiency, and chronic pain. Prominent neuropsychiatric and sleep symptoms (depression, anxiety, insomnia) accompany these conditions. Overall it captures a general internal-medicine baseline in older adults rather than a single disease process.

phenotypes.label: Falls, bleeding, and bone fragility
phenotypes.description: Pattern of geriatric frailty with bone health deficits (vitamin D deficiency, osteoporosis) and fall-related injury, including bleeding and postconcussive symptoms. Patients also show cardiopulmonary complaints (orthopnea, wheezing, shortness of breath) and nonspecific symptoms such as pain and nausea/vomiting/diarrhea, with coexisting anxiety and depression common in this group.

phenotypes.label: Neuropsychiatric symptoms with injury in dementia
phenotypes.description: Prominent neuropsychiatric symptoms—anxiety, insomnia, and distress—co-occur with pain and nonspecific discomfort. These features appear alongside documented injury, consistent with elevated fall/trauma risk in the setting of early dementia and behavioral disturbance. Memory complaints and depression are secondary, with other comorbidities present but not driving the pattern.

phenotypes.label: Postconcussion syndrome
phenotypes.description: Clear focus on postconcussion syndrome, with frequent co-occurring anxiety and depressive disorders, insomnia, and pain complaints. This reflects persistent symptoms after mild traumatic brain injury with prominent mood and somatic features.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are a mix of generic symptoms (chest pain, wheezing, dyspnea, nausea) and common comorbidities seen broadly across patients. There is no unifying clinical theme across these conditions, so this pattern is not useful for identifying a specific phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Anxiety/depression with insomnia in dementia
phenotypes.description: Prominent mood and anxiety disorders with insomnia in the context of dementia. Depressive episodes, generalized anxiety, sleep disturbance, and pain co-occur with cognitive symptoms (dementia, amnesia, neurological deficits), alongside nonspecific GI and cardiopulmonary complaints. Metabolic comorbidities (diabetes, obesity, hyperlipidemia) are present but secondary to the neuropsychiatric pattern.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are a blend of common chronic comorbidities (hypertension, diabetes, hyperlipidemia, obesity) and generic acute symptoms (chest pain, nausea, shortness of breath) without a unifying clinical theme. This mix does not define a specific condition pattern within the cohort.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are a nonspecific mix of common comorbidities (hypertension, hyperlipidemia) and generic symptoms (chest pain, shortness of breath, pain, anxiety), with scattered items such as postconcussion syndrome and seizures. There is no unifying clinical theme, so this pattern is not useful for defining a clinical phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Unused low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions mix generic symptoms (pain, nausea, chest pain) with common chronic comorbidities (hypertension, type 2 diabetes, obesity) and scattered entries like osteoporosis and influenza. There is no unifying clinical story, so this is not a usable phenotype.
